inference_rules.healthcare: Folder name or files contain medical/HCC/HIPAA/RCM/clinical indicators
inference_rules.deployment_rule: healthcare projects → AWS (HIPAA-eligible infrastructure); non-healthcare → standard cloud

projects.name: codemedgroup
inference_signals: app.py: CodeMed Group Platform v2.0 — HCC risk adjustment + medical billing

services: AWS Elastic Beanstalk or ECS Fargate (Flask app hosting)
Amazon RDS for PostgreSQL (replace SQLite — WAL/FTS5 → pg_fts)
AWS HealthLake (FHIR-compliant clinical data store for PHI)
AWS KMS (encryption at rest for audit_log and users tables)
AWS WAF + Shield (API rate limiting, DDoS protection)
deployment_plan.notes: BAA with AWS required before storing any PHI. Enable VPC isolation for RDS.

projects.name: codemed-app
inference_signals: Folder name contains 'codemed' — known healthcare brand
Contains Procfile + railway.json — deployment artifacts present but not yet live

services: AWS Elastic Beanstalk (lift-and-shift from Railway Procfile)
Amazon RDS for PostgreSQL (production-grade DB replace SQLite)
deployment_plan.notes: Merge or archive — appears to be a staging copy of codemedgroup/. Confirm if this is a separate deployment target or duplicate before provisioning AWS resources.

projects.name: nextjs
inference_signals: package.json: @anthropic-ai/sdk, @supabase/supabase-js, stripe — CodeMed AI SaaS frontend
app/admin/: admin portal — consistent with regulated healthcare platform

services: AWS Amplify or CloudFront + S3 (Next.js static + SSR hosting)
AWS Lambda@Edge (SSR for Next.js App Router pages)
Amazon Cognito (replace Supabase Auth — HIPAA-eligible identity provider)
AWS Secrets Manager (Stripe keys, Anthropic API key)
deployment_plan.notes: Supabase is not HIPAA-eligible without a BAA. Migrate auth to Amazon Cognito or a HIPAA-covered identity provider before handling PHI in frontend sessions.
